Group A: Arief/Moeda win over Osman/Ferdi (7-2) |
Group B: Dian/Fariz win over Syakhrul/Akbar (7-1)

Group A: Arief/Moeda win over Giffa/Mursal (7-4),  Giffa/Mursal win over Osman/Ferdi (7-3) |
Group B: Dian/Fariz win over Zes/Rahmat (7-6)

Memed/Heri earned a convincing 7-2 victory over Syakhrul/Akbar by dominating the return game, controlling errors, and producing a decisive five-game winning run after the score reached 2-2. Memed/Heri won 34 of the match's 53 points, captured 78% of receiving points, broke serve in all five of Syakhrul/Akbar's service games, and committed only eight unforced errors compared with eighteen from their opponents. Although the winner count was relatively close at 7-5, the ten-error advantage proved far more decisive and was reflected in the app-reported aggressive margins of +8 and -7. The main area for Memed/Heri to improve is service effectiveness: despite landing 85% of first serves, they won only 45% of first-serve points and held just two of four service games. Overall, the match was won through exceptional return pressure, superior rally stability, better decision-making, and much stronger error control

Group A: Arief/Moeda win over Dian/Fathur (7-1) |
Group B: Syahrul/Akbar win over Zes/Rahmat (7-5)

Memed/Heri delivered a dominant performance, defeating Zes/Rahmat 7–2 in around 26 minutes. Although Zes/Rahmat had a slightly higher first-serve percentage at 80% compared to 76%, Memed/Heri were far more effective after the serve, winning 92% of first-serve points and 100% of second-serve points. They also controlled the return game, winning 60% of receiving points and converting 3 of 5 break points, while Zes/Rahmat created no break chances. Memed/Heri led clearly in total points won, 34–13, produced more winners, 8–3, and forced Zes/Rahmat into too many errors.
First results are in from Group A: Osman/Ferdi with a 7-3 win over Dian/Fathur.
First results are in from Group B: Fariz/Dian opened with a 7-3 win over Memed/Heri.
Random pairs draw complete — 8 pairs split into Group A and Group B for round-robin group play.
